spec.emotional_state: frustrated about a small thing today
spec.label: B02_consec_d1

profile.profession: Medical student preparing for NEET PG
profile.personality: earnest, slightly anxious about exams, opens up about study stress

transcript.content: Hi Aarti, I'm Miss Maya. Honestly, the monsoon rains make me feel relaxed. How's your NEET PG prep going these days?
Honestly, it's been a bit up and down. The monsoon makes me sleepy, and I can't focus much. Aajcha typhoon made me lose my internet for hours, so I couldn't study. Wahi, I'm trying my best.
"Up and down is normal, exam prep is tough. The typhoon sounds frustrating. What are you planning to do today to stay on track?"

I thought of revising anatomy today, but my mind isn't cooperating. Maybe I'll cook something to take my mind off, like my favorite dal. But I feel bad about not studying. Have you ever felt like this before?
It happens, your mind needs rest too. Cooking is a good break, and dal is comforting. What's your secret to making it taste extra good?

I add a lot of jeera and green chilies when I make dal. It gives it extra flavor. But honestly, most of the time I just cook it to feel like I'm doing something productive. Even if it's not studying, at least I'm not completely slacking. But now I feel guilty for talking about food instead of books.
I get that, food is a small win when your brain is heavy. Jeera and green chilies are a great combo. What’s one thing you could do today to feel even half as productive as cooking?
Maybe I could solve a few practice questions while the dal is cooking. That way, I’m not wasting time. But I don’t know… I just feel so stuck today. Kahi chalu karyasathi kichha karaycha lagat.
I get how stuck feels, especially with exams looming. Practice questions while cooking, that’s smart. What’s ONE small question you could try right now?
Hmm, I could try a sample MCQ from my anatomy flashcards… just one. But I’m worried I’ll get it wrong and lose more motivation. Should I even try?
One question is better than none, no need to worry. Mistakes help us learn. What’s the first thing that pops into your mind about the question?

The question is about the blood supply of the liver. I know it has two sources — the hepatic artery and the portal vein. But I’m not sure which one supplies oxygenated blood. This sounds familiar, but my brain feels like it’s underwater. Do you know the answer?
The hepatic artery brings oxygenated blood to the liver, you’re on the right track. What’s ONE thing you’ll do after this to stay steady?
I’ll go make my dal and try one more question while it simmers. Maybe the smell of jeera will wake up my brain a bit. Thanks for not letting me quit, Maya.
You're welcome, the jeera will do its magic! What’s the next question you’ll try?
Maybe the one about the brachial plexus — roots and nerves. I always mix up C5-C6 vs C5-C7. Let me think... C5 and C6 form the upper trunk? Or is it C5-C7? My head is still foggy.
Upper trunk is C5 and C6, you’re getting close! What’s the next part you’ll tackle while your dal is ready?

I'll try to recall the main branches — like the musculocutaneous from C5-C7. But I think I should stop here and not overwork my brain. Thanks for keeping me going, Maya. I'll try again tomorrow. Time to eat my dal and take a short break. Talk to you later.
You’re doing great, take that break, your brain deserves it. Talk to you soon!

moments.text: Preparing for NEET PG is stressful
I think I need a cooking teacher, like a tutor for recipes!
I should balance study and cooking better—otherwise, I'll just end up with more stress!
